Title: KAI2-dependent signaling controls vegetative reproduction in Marchantia polymorpha through activation of LOG-mediated cytokinin synthesis

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: meristem region of gemma; gemma initial cells; initiating gemmae; slime papillae; gemma cup floor cells
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: promotes gemma cup formation, gemma formation, and aspects of thallus growth during vegetative reproduction
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: final activation step of active cytokinin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: hydrolyzes cytokinin riboside 5'-monophosphates to release active cytokinin nucleobases and ribose 5-phosphate
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in meristem region of gemma, rhizoid initial cells, initiating gemma cups, upper thallus surface, gemma initial cells, initiating gemmae, slime papillae, and gemma cup floor cells; KAI2-dependent in meristem region, gemma initial cells, and initiating gemmae
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: terminal activation step; not established as rate-limiting in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native MpLOG promoter (1 kb and 5 kb) for EGFP-NLS reporter; endogenous gene analyzed in WT and mutants
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: continuous light at 22°C; vermiculite versus 1/2 B5 medium strongly affected gemma cup phenotype; exogenous 6-benzylaminopurine rescued mutant defects; (−)-GR24 induced expression via KAI2 signaling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, DDBJ DRA019200 (BioProject PRJDB18746); cytokinin metabolite profiling by UPLC-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MpLOG is positively regulated by KAI2-dependent signaling and induced by (−)-GR24 in a KAI2A/MAX2/SMXL-dependent manner. 2. CRISPR loss-of-function mutants reduced active cytokinin levels, especially tZ to undetectable levels, while cytokinin ribotide precursors accumulated strongly. 3. Mplog mutants showed reduced gemma cup formation and fewer gemmae, and these defects were rescued by exogenous cytokinin. 4. Genetic analysis with Mpsmxl placed MpLOG downstream of KAI2-dependent signaling. 5. Cell-type-specific MpLOG expression links KAI2 signaling to local cytokinin production during vegetative reproduction.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; qPCR; promoter-EGFP reporter analysis; cytokinin quantification by UPLC-MS/MS; genetic interaction/double mutant analysis; exogenous cytokinin rescue
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes active cytokinin accumulation and thereby promotes gemma cup formation, gemma formation, and proper thallus growth orientation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mplog mutants showed upward thallus growth defects; higher BA concentrations were required to fully rescue thallus angle, suggesting additional pathways affect this trait
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: could be used to manipulate cytokinin-dependent vegetative propagation by targeting spatial MpLOG activity or stacking with upstream KAI2 signaling and downstream GCAM1 modules
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: M. polymorpha has a single LOG gene. Active cytokinins remained detectable in Mplog mutants, supporting an additional LOG-independent cytokinin synthesis pathway.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB transcription factor; ortholog of Arabidopsis RAX genes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in gemma initial cells, initiating gemmae, and slime papillae at the bottom of gemma cups
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: vegetative reproduction downstream of cytokinin signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: acts downstream of cytokinin as a transcriptional activator/regulator of gemma cup and gemma formation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: cytokinin synthesized by MpLOG; KAI2-dependent signaling indirectly via MpLOG
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: continuous light at 22°C; exogenous cytokinin induced expression
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native GCAM1 promoter (~5 kb) for EGFPNLS reporter
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: promotes vegetative reproduction through gemma cup and gemma formation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: acts downstream of MpLOG-derived cytokinin to promote gemma cup and gemma formation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: master regulator of vegetative reproduction output downstream of cytokinin
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR after cytokinin treatment; promoter-reporter imaging in WT and Mplog background
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GCAM1 expression is induced by cytokinin treatment. 2. GCAM1 reporter signal is present in gemma initial cells and initiating gemmae in WT. 3. GCAM1 expression in these cells is lost in Mplog mutants, placing GCAM1 downstream of MpLOG-mediated cytokinin synthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful downstream target for engineering vegetative propagation together with cytokinin biosynthesis control
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper treats GCAM1 as the master regulator of vegetative reproduction, but not as a direct regulator of cytokinin biosynthesis itself.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SMXL family suppressor protein with EAR motif; weak homology to ClpB-type chaperonins
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: cytokinin biosynthesis via repression of KAI2-dependent output
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: negative regulation; proteolysis-sensitive suppressor downstream of KAI2A/MAX2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses MpLOG-associated cytokinin biosynthesis output; degradation relieves repression
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: KAI2 ligand/KL perception by MpKAI2A; synthetic (−)-GR24/rac-GR24 in experimental systems
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: (−)-GR24/rac-GR24 treatments used to activate pathway; continuous light at 22°C
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: proSMXL-SMXLd53 used to express degradation-resistant MpSMXL
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: promotes vegetative reproduction when biosynthesis is elevated
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: suppresses cytokinin accumulation and vegetative reproduction outputs when stabilized; loss of function enhances gemma number phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: key suppressor whose degradation is required for KAI2-dependent activation of MpLOG and cytokinin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: degradation-resistant overexpression line; loss-of-function mutants; cytokinin quantification; genetic epistasis with Mplog
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Stabilized MpSMXL reduced MpLOG expression and lowered active cytokinin levels. 2. Mpsmxl mutants showed enhanced gemma production phenotypes. 3. Mpsmxl Mplog double mutants resembled Mplog, indicating MpLOG acts downstream of MpSMXL for vegetative reproduction traits.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq context from KAI2 pathway analyses; DDBJ DRA019200 (BioProject PRJDB18746)
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Stabilized MpSMXL disrupts vegetative reproduction and cytokinin-associated development
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: a leverage point for modulating endogenous cytokinin output through upstream signaling repression/de-repression
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MpSMXL is a central signaling suppressor rather than a classic transcription factor; included as a regulatory core gene because direct genetic evidence links it to cytokinin output via MpLOG.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: reported previously to localize more broadly than the cell-type-specific MpLOG output
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: activation of cytokinin biosynthesis through KAI2-dependent signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: positive signaling activation via ligand perception leading to MAX2-dependent degradation of MpSMXL
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: (−)-GR24 or rac-GR24 treatments; continuous light at 22°C; vermiculite versus medium affected phenotype readout
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: local developmental sites where MpLOG is induced
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: positively regulates cytokinin-mediated gemma cup and gemma formation by activating MpLOG expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: upstream pathway receptor essential for induction of MpLOG in specific cell types
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant analysis; qPCR of MpLOG after (−)-GR24; cytokinin rescue of mutant phenotypes; promoter reporter comparison in WT vs Mpkai2a
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MpKAI2A is required for (−)-GR24-induced MpLOG expression. 2. Mpkai2a mutants phenocopy Mplog defects in gemma cup/gemma formation and are rescued by exogenous cytokinin. 3. KAI2A is required for MpLOG expression specifically in meristematic and gemma-forming cell types.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq context from KAI2-pathway activation, DDBJ DRA019200 (BioProject PRJDB18746)
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function impairs vegetative reproduction and thallus growth orientation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful upstream module to spatially control cytokinin synthesis and vegetative propagation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Primary signaling component rather than direct cytokinin metabolism gene; included because the paper's core causal chain centers on KAI2A→MpLOG→cytokinin→GCAM1.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: activation of cytokinin biosynthesis through KAI2-dependent signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: positive signaling through proteolysis of MpSMXL downstream of KAI2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: promotes MpLOG expression indirectly by enabling MpSMXL degradation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: activated following KL/(−)-GR24 perception by KAI2A; rac-GR24 in AtD14 transgenic system
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: (−)-GR24/rac-GR24; continuous light at 22°C; vermiculite versus medium
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter used for AtD14 in sensitized transcriptomic system, not for MpMAX2 itself
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: local developmental sites where MpLOG is induced
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: required for KAI2-dependent activation of MpLOG and downstream cytokinin-mediated vegetative reproduction
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: essential transducer enabling de-repression of MpLOG and cytokinin synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: loss-of-function mutant analysis; qPCR of MpLOG; cytokinin rescue of mutant phenotypes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MpMAX2 is required for normal MpLOG expression and for (−)-GR24-induced MpLOG activation. 2. Mpmax2 mutants show gemma cup and thallus defects resembling Mplog. 3. Exogenous cytokinin rescues major vegetative reproduction defects of Mpmax2, placing cytokinin downstream.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq context from KAI2-pathway activation, DDBJ DRA019200 (BioProject PRJDB18746)
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function impairs vegetative reproduction and thallus growth orientation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: upstream signaling control point for modulating cytokinin-dependent vegetative propagation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core regulatory gene because the Results section experimentally connects MpMAX2 to MpLOG expression and cytokinin-dependent phenotypes.
